name: Vegetarian Stuffed Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 1/2 cups salted water to a boil. Add 1 cup rice, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes (or until all liquid is absorbed).
2. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a frying pan. Add minced garlic and sauté for 3 minutes, stirring frequently so the garlic doesn't burn.
3. Add onion, celery, and carrots to the pan. Sauté for an additional 5-7 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and mix with the rice.
5. Stir in the seasonings (all the dried seasonings, plus salt and pepper).
6. Mix in the beans with 2 tablespoons of the reserved liquid.
7. Cut the tops off the green peppers and remove the seeds.
8. Brush a shallow baking dish with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
9. Fill peppers with bean and rice mixture.
10. Ladle seasoned diced tomatoes over top of each pepper.
11. Top each pepper with a tablespoon of reserved liquid.
12. Cover the dish and roast in a 350°F oven for 35-45 minutes, or until peppers are tender.
